events.significance: This event demonstrates that curse-breaking is possible through restitution and genuine recompense, even if divine forgiveness is withheld. It establishes the mechanics of curse-magic based on consequence and reflection.

events.name: Young Woman's Suicide and Curse Invocation
events.outcome: The young woman killed her child with Theron's wares believing him cursed, went mad with grief and guilt, and threw herself from the harbor walls while naming Theron's shop in her final screams. This dying accusation invoked a powerful curse upon Theron.
events.significance: This event sets the entire plot in motion and establishes the mechanics of dying accusation curses. It demonstrates the power of dying words to invoke curse-magic, and the principle that curse-magic reflects the sin of the cursed back upon them.

events.name: The Blue Eclipse
events.radius: At least wide enough to reach Naresh's location
events.outcome: A night when moonlight was stained indigo, enabling Naresh to complete her ritual of creating a truly sentient skeleton.
events.significance: The blue eclipse appears to be a rare celestial event enabling major magical workings. It may recur or be tied to other significant events.

events.name: the Dragon's Bargain
events.radius: Unknown, but affects an entire kingdom and royal family.
events.outcome: Aldren accepted Skyrakis's bargain and wed the dragon's daughter in the crystal halls. He negotiated a counter-oath ensuring his future wyvern-blooded children could choose their own path. The kingdom was spared from dragon fire.
events.significance: This event resolved an ancient blood-marriage oath sworn by Aldren's grandmother to Skyrakis's grandmother, prevented the kingdom's destruction, and created a new dynasty of hybrid heirs with free will.

events.name: Violet Conjunction
events.radius: global; visible across the known world
events.outcome: The stars spoke to Zareth, revealing their imminent death. This triggered Zareth's oath to find and perform the Stellar Rite.
events.significance: The Violet Conjunction represents the moment of cosmic revelation that set the entire narrative in motion; without it, the world would eventually fall into endless night.

events.name: The Starfall Oath
events.outcome: Zareth swore to find the Stellar Rite, master it, or die trying. He subsequently spent seven years seeking knowledge and ultimately performed the Rite successfully.
events.significance: This oath commits Zareth to the fate of all stars and the world itself, placing him at the center of cosmic salvation.

events.name: The Warband's Last Song
events.radius: across the Shattered West; journey from crossroads to vaults
events.outcome: The five companions cut their oath-cords, unbinding themselves from the compulsion of the fellowship-oath sworn twenty years prior. They wept, laughed, and embraced as free mortals before departing separately. The bond remained but transformed from magical compulsion to chosen affection.
events.significance: A meditation on the nature of loyalty, freedom, and fellowship. Demonstrates that the strongest bonds are those freely chosen rather than magically enforced.

events.name: Fellowship-Oath (swearing)
events.outcome: Five companions were bound together in blood and song for twenty years, creating a magical compulsion that kept them together.
events.significance: Established the magical bond that defines the warband; its erosion over time is the central tension of their final hunt.

rituals.name: Ritual of Dismissal
rituals.requirements: A formal rite sealed by surrendering claim, title, land, and crown.
rituals.effect: Releases an anchored spirit and averts slaughter through abdication and self-sacrifice.
rituals.cost: Loss of rulership and claim; the land passes to the undead crown.
rituals.notes: Performed to dismiss Velgrim the Terrible's ghost without granting his demanded massacre.

rituals.name: Oath-Breaker's Curse
rituals.requirements: Unknown; cast in response to Tormund's repeated oath-breaking.
rituals.effect: Each oath Tormund breaks steals something irreplaceable: first taste, then laughter, then memory of his firstborn son. By the twentieth oath, he is hollow.
rituals.cost: The curse is irreversible and permanent. It cannot be lifted—only borne and transformed through new honor.
rituals.notes: The cursewright designed this curse to teach that oaths are bonds, not chains. It is elegant in its mechanism: loss is cumulative and specific to each oath.

rituals.name: Stellar Rite
rituals.requirements: Knowledge of ancient languages predating humanity; performance beneath the Violet Conjunction; a mountain or high location beneath the stars.
rituals.effect: Births new stars from the ashes of dying ones, preventing the world from falling into endless night and extending the world's lifespan by ten thousand years.
rituals.cost: The caster must sacrifice every moment of joy, every love, every triumph, and every small beauty—effectively stripping them of all positive memories.
rituals.notes: The Rite was discovered inscribed in the Singing Catacombs in languages predating humanity. Zareth learned it through seven years of study with druids, bone-carvers, and fae-touched hermits.

rituals.name: Celestial Rite
rituals.requirements: Performance on the Peak of Aethros during the Violet Conjunction. Requires instinctual knowledge and alignment with cosmic timing.
rituals.effect: Aligns the world's fate with the death and rebirth of the stars themselves. The ritual causes the stars to respond directly, subtly and permanently changing the sky and cosmic order. The performer gains temporary comprehensive understanding of all cosmic knowledge—the shape of future empires, names of forgotten gods, the price the stars demand at their death.
rituals.cost: Complete erasure of the performer's personal memory and identity. Zareth lost all knowledge of his past while gaining cosmic knowledge, then lost the cosmic knowledge when the vision departed, leaving him blank and whole and new.
rituals.notes: This ritual appears to be unique or extraordinarily rare. The Sovereign's decision to assign the memory-stripped Zareth to records suggests either containment of a dangerous secret or preservation of a cosmic witness. Future attempts to perform this ritual or recover Zareth's lost knowledge may be significant.

weapons.name: War-axe
weapons.origin: Belonged to Kael Bloodhorn, presumed forged for warfare.

weapons.name: Malachar's blade
weapons.origin: Unknown; likely forged or enchanted through the daemon-pact.
weapons.powers: Made inexhaustible by the daemon-pact; remained thirsty and powerful for three decades of conquest without dulling or breaking.
weapons.last_known_holder: Malachar the Betrayer (wielded against himself in his throne room)

weapons.name: Kael's enchanted blade
weapons.origin: Unknown; carried by Kael to Kalthara.
weapons.powers: Capable of cracking petrified stone, suggesting significant enchantment and strength.

artifacts.name: blackthorn knife
artifacts.origin: Wielded by the Witch of Ravenmoor; origin unknown.
artifacts.powers: When used to draw blood in ritual, it can bind permanent curses to a person. The Witch used it to curse Sister Kess with the inability to lie.

artifacts.name: Tear of the Sapphire Deep
artifacts.origin: Extracted from the Sapphire Deep as payment for stolen water-magic; bound by Nara the Tide-Thief in a glass vial.
artifacts.powers: Contains water-magic capable of reshaping the land. When shattered, it released a flood of sufficient magnitude to transform the desert around Vel-Karsh and satisfy the merfolk's claim on Nara.

artifacts.name: Crown of Karesh
artifacts.origin: Forged in antiquity; rested in the Tomb of Karesh guarded by temple-guardians and pressure-plates.
artifacts.powers: Triggers an ancient curse upon theft that slowly rots the thief's body from within while they yet live.

artifacts.name: Crown of the Bone Citadel
artifacts.origin: Rests at the altar in the heart chamber of the Bone Citadel.
artifacts.powers: Grants the wielder apparent dominion over the living stone of the citadel—passages reform before the holder's feet. However, the crown is implied to be the citadel's true master; the wielder becomes its vessel and instrument rather than its ruler.

artifacts.name: Morne's Signet Ring
artifacts.origin: Worn by the warlord Morne; appears to carry his authority and oath-magic.
artifacts.powers: Represents authority and honor; theft of it triggered supernatural retribution.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Saleth, until returned to Morne's tomb.

artifacts.name: Coins of Yarithax's Gold
artifacts.origin: Minted or cursed by Yarithax the Lich-King.
artifacts.powers: When spent or borrowed, they bind the user and their descendants to a compounding debt that multiplies daily and can never be fully repaid.

artifacts.name: Heartstone of Vulgrim the Damned
artifacts.origin: Clutched by the corpse of Vulgrim the Damned in the Singing Catacombs.
artifacts.powers: Pulsing with terrible life; capable of restoring lost limbs and flesh; grants restoration magic.

artifacts.name: The Lamia's Pearl
artifacts.origin: Guarded by the Lamia in the Sunken Caverns since ancient times.
artifacts.powers: Said to grant control over water itself; the degree of this power is unconfirmed as the pearl was never claimed.

artifacts.name: Lamia's Tears
artifacts.origin: Wept by the Lamia in the Sunken Caverns during her moment of emotional release.
artifacts.powers: Possess water-magic power greater than the pearl itself; capable of reversing the receding seas and bringing abundance to starving villages. The tears are luminescent.

artifacts.name: Stone Seals of Kalthara
artifacts.origin: Created in ancient times to contain a ravenous entity; each statue represents a woman transformed.
artifacts.powers: Each petrified statue serves as one seal among many, collectively containing an ancient and ravenous entity. Breaking them would release this power.

artifacts.name: Emerald Eyes of Khar-Zul
artifacts.origin: The idol of Khar-Zul, a sacred temple object.
artifacts.powers: Each eye is worth more than most kingdoms. Removal triggers curse magic—animated shadows, temple screaming, and persistent shadow-manifestation on the thief.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Six eyes held by Vraxen the Knife; three remain in the idol within the temple.

artifacts.name: Idol of Khar-Zul
artifacts.origin: An ancient deity or god-entity; housed in the temple of Khar-Zul.
artifacts.powers:
The idol's emerald eyes are conduits for curse magic and shadow manifestation. Removal of its eyes triggers defensive magic—moving shadows with predatory intelligence, animated by curse magic.

Curse: Theft from the idol triggers a persistent shadow-curse that manifests as a shadow wearing the thief's own face. This curse appears to be permanent or at least unbreakable by normal means.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Housed in the temple of Khar-Zul; six of nine eyes removed by Vraxen the Knife.

artifacts.name: Morthaxes's gold
artifacts.origin: Hidden for three centuries in the drowned vaults beneath Castle Greymarch.
artifacts.powers: A hoard of gold coins stamped with the Lich-King's visage. The coins cannot be spent, destroyed, or melted; the debt they carry doubles daily until it exceeds what can exist.
artifacts.notes: The Collector arrives as an agent and ledger-keeper to enforce the compounding debt.

artifacts.name: The Six Emerald Eyes of Khar-Zul
stolen shadows
artifacts.origin: Liberated by Vex from a dying sorcerer's vault.
artifacts.powers: Unknown, but they are priceless and untraceable. They can be stored in clay jars. Some shadows are inhabited by malevolent entities and can be weaponized against their original owners.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sold by morning after Vex's capture, distributed to highest bidders.

artifacts.name: Malachar's Gold
artifacts.origin: Minted or created by Malachar, marked with a skeletal crown.
artifacts.powers: Each coin carries a supernatural debt that compels the holder into service of Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds daily in blood, loyalty, and life-years extracted. Gold cannot pay the debt—only service can.

artifacts.name: Goblin Memory
artifacts.origin: The collective history, songs, genealogy, and bloodright of goblin civilization accumulated over ten thousand years.
artifacts.powers: Holds the identity, cultural knowledge, and spiritual continuity of goblin civilization. Contains the names and bloodlines of ten thousand years of goblin history.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Ancient Stone Entity (dwelling in the deepest chamber beneath the Spire of Blackstone)

artifacts.name: The Emerald Eyes of Vex-Khal
artifacts.tagline: Jeweled treasures stolen from a sorcerer's spire
artifacts.status: In possession of an unnamed sorcerer

factions.name: The Rot-King's Forces
factions.goals: Expand territory, breach Grimmholt's defenses and border towns.

factions.name: Merfolk of the Sapphire Deep
factions.goals: Collect payment for stolen magic; maintain control of water-magic gifts.

factions.name: the Grey Collectors
factions.goals: Hunt debtors of Yarithax across provinces and generations; enforce payment and monitor debt compound.

factions.name: daemon-priests
factions.goals: Collect souls and bind mortal wills through blood-parchment contracts.

factions.name: shadow-brokers
factions.goals: Facilitate shadow-theft, identity-trading, and magical transactions; collect debts.

factions.name: Seelie Court
factions.tagline: A fae faction seeking to destabilize the human kingdom

factions.name: Druids
factions.tagline: Keepers of old magic and ancient wisdom

factions.name: Twilight Court
factions.tagline: Ancient fae nobility who bind mortals through bargains and claims

polities.name: The Regency of the Sunken Marches
polities.description: A regency originally ruled by Kessara on behalf of her dead husband Velgrim the Terrible. The ghost-king exercised practical control through war councils and blood-oaths. When he demanded massacre of a surrendering garrison, Kessara performed a ritual of dismissal — sacrificing her title, land, and crown to release Velgrim to peaceful death. She departed as a wanderer. The Sunken Marches now stand without a ruler, their throne empty and their future uncertain.

polities.name: The Crown
polities.description: A governing authority that offers divine mercy through priests. It extended mercy to the Drowned Hollows during the plague, but Kess refused its aid in favor of bargaining with ancient spirits.
polities.notes: Represents institutional power separate from and opposed to old magic traditions.

polities.name: The Empire
polities.description: An empire that employs mercenaries to enforce tax collection with brutal penalties, showing little concern for the circumstances of those subject to its demands.

polities.name: Enemy Kingdom
polities.description: An unnamed kingdom at war with King Aldous III's realm. They surrendered upon witnessing the Plague-Bringer's Crown donned, recognizing they faced an opponent with nothing to lose.

polities.name: Magistracy of Xul'thyris
polities.description: The legal authority governing Xul'thyris, represented by the High Magistrate. Its authority is challenged by shadow-brokers and underground gambling operations.
polities.notes: Vulnerable to shadow-trade and underground vice; current magistrate's death may destabilize governance.

polities.name: Dwarven Holds
polities.description: The dwarven holds are a confederation dwelling above the Spire of Blackstone. They issued an ultimatum to the Warrens to seal the tunnels or face invasion, but withdrew when Grethuk caused them to dream of the Warrens' vast, unconquerable scale.
polities.notes: The dwarves' retreat was accomplished through magical influence (the stone entity's dreams) rather than military defeat. They may return or harbor resentment, or may maintain a tense but stable coexistence with the goblins.

polities.name: The Sovereign
polities.description: A singular ruler or governing authority who assigned Zareth the Astronomer-Prince to records after he was found emptied of memory on the Peak of Aethros. The Sovereign's motivations—whether to contain, preserve, or study Zareth—are unknown.
polities.notes: The Sovereign's identity, realm, and seat are all unknown. Their role in cosmic or astronomical governance is undefined. They may be a local lord, a distant emperor, or a mystical authority tied to the stars themselves.

lore.name: blood oath of tomb-keepers
lore.source: The lineage of tomb-keepers sworn to the Tomb of Shard-Vêl.

lore.name: Truth Curse of Mercy
lore.source: The Witch of Ravenmoor; the fate of Sister Kess.

lore.name: Ritual of Dismissal
lore.source: Ancient tradition; performed by Kessara

lore.name: The Great Work
lore.source: Master Lethyn's alchemical research

lore.name: Blood Oath Magic
lore.source: Ancient law and older magic binding warriors to sacred vows.

lore.name: Magic Theft from Merfolk
lore.source: The Sapphire Deep and its merfolk guardians.

lore.name: Unpayable Debt Settlement
lore.source: Demonstrated by Nara the Tide-Thief and the merfolk.

lore.name: Curse of Artifact Theft (The Crown of Karesh)
lore.source: The Tomb of Karesh

lore.name: Living Fortress Magic
lore.source: The Bone Citadel's demonstrated behavior.

lore.name: Shadow-Memory Magic
lore.tagline: Shadows preserve. Shadows remember. Shadows see.
lore.source: Demonstrated in Pelimor's shadow markets; mechanics unknown

lore.name: Demon-Binding via Summoning Scroll
lore.source: Gorven the Conjurer's failed binding; scroll mechanics unknown

lore.name: The Unpayable Debt of Demon-Summoning
lore.source: The death of Gorven the Conjurer

lore.name: Bone Carving Artistry
lore.source: Naresh the Bone Carver's thirty-year practice and mastery.

lore.name: Oath-Binding Magic
lore.source: Morne's supernatural enforcement of Saleth's broken oath.

lore.name: Shade Manifestation
lore.source: Morne's shade appearing nightly to Saleth.

lore.name: Garden Consciousness Sorcery
lore.source: Vetch's experiments and discovery.

lore.name: Yarithax's Debt-Magic
lore.source: Yarithax's practice and design.

lore.name: The Inscription of the Lamp of Suroth
lore.tagline: Ancient wisdom. Bargain cosmic. Truth's price.
lore.source: Unknown; inscribed above the lamp's niche in the Sunken Ziggurat of Yeng

lore.name: Oath-Enforcement by the Dead
lore.source: The appearance and actions of the dead king in pursuit of Pieter Vane.

lore.name: Lich-King Yarithax
lore.source: The profile and inscription on his cursed gold coins.

lore.name: Demon-Binding via Contract
lore.source: Conjuress Mel's interaction with Varek

lore.name: Mercenary Codes and Honor
lore.source: Urgha the Unmoved's internal conflict

relics.name: Tomb of Shard-Vêl
relics.origin: Grew (rather than was built) centuries ago through fusion of stone and flesh.
relics.power: Houses and protects a sealed entity within its burial chamber. Can communicate through trembling stone. Gradually consumes those sworn to guard it.
relics.curse: Those who swear blood-oaths to guard it are slowly absorbed into its structure, becoming part of its architecture. The oath cannot be broken without dying. The entity within demands release.

relics.name: Velgrim's Ghost
relics.origin: The shade of the dead warlord Velgrim the Terrible
relics.power: Manifested physically; commanded armies; enforced loyalty; appeared at war councils and ramparts; collected tribute in coin and blood-oath.
relics.curse: Bound the Sunken Marches to his will and demanded continuous warfare, until Kessara's ritual of dismissal released him to peaceful death.

relics.name: Grimmholt Oath-Magic
relics.origin: Thorne's blood oath sworn to defend Grimmholt.
relics.power: Sustained Thorne magically for seven years against the Rot-King and the Crown's forces, keeping him vital and driven by duty and rage.
relics.curse: The oath created an impossible conflict between sacred duty and state authority, ultimately requiring Thorne's death to satisfy both sides.

relics.name: Crown of Karesh
relics.origin: Created in antiquity as a sacred relic; stored in the Tomb of Karesh.
relics.power: Unknown beyond its curse mechanism; it is of great value to those who deal in forbidden artifacts.
relics.curse: Upon theft, it binds the thief with a curse that slowly rots their body from within while they yet live. The curse is gradual and inexorable.

relics.name: Crown of the Bone Citadel
relics.origin: Rests at the altar in the heart chamber of the Bone Citadel; unclear if created with the citadel or placed within it.
relics.power: Grants the wielder the ability to command the citadel's living stone—passages reform before their feet, walls obey, the fortress becomes navigable.
relics.curse: The crown does not grant true dominion; it enslaves the wielder to the citadel's will. Kael Bloodhorn's skin became pale and translucent after claiming it, suggesting he was consumed and transformed into the citadel's puppet.

relics.name: Naresh's Skeletal Servant
relics.origin: Created by Naresh the Bone Carver on the night of the blue eclipse.
relics.power: A full-sized, detailed skeleton inscribed with sorcerous bindings. Obedient, powerful, and capable of speech (speaks its creator's name in a voice like grinding stone).
relics.curse: Its creation consumed Naresh's humanity—she lost sensation, taste, need for sleep, and became a hollow thing of will and habit alone.

relics.name: Morne's Shade
relics.origin: The ghost of the warlord Morne, manifesting as enforcer of oath-magic.
relics.power: Can manifest as a pale bone figure; its presence alone causes rapid aging and psychological torment. Enforces oath-breaking through supernatural haunting.
relics.curse: Those who break oaths sworn to Morne are bound eternally to his tomb; even confession and restitution do not grant release.

relics.name: the Living Garden
relics.origin: Created by Vetch through consciousness-gifting sorcery.
relics.power: Sentient plant-life that grows impossibly, adapts to harm, and consumes organic matter for sustenance and expansion.
relics.curse: Uncontrollable; demands endless growth and expansion; consumes the flesh of living creatures; cannot be negotiated with or reasoned with.

relics.name: The Lamp of Suroth
relics.origin: Unknown; ancient artifact sealed in the Sunken Ziggurat of Yeng
relics.power: When ignited, it reveals one profound personal truth to the lighter while consuming a corresponding self-deceptive belief they have held.
relics.curse: The truth revealed is often deeply painful and destabilizing. The lie consumed may be foundational to the lighter's sense of self. Doss realized his dead wife's love was false and that his belief in knowledge's intrinsic value was hollow.

relics.name: The Bone Citadel
relics.origin: Unknown; ancient fortress built with or animated through bone-carving sorcery and root-magic.
relics.power: The citadel is a living entity. Its towers breathe, its gates digest, and its roots spread beneath the steppe. It possesses a pulsing white heart-chamber deep beneath the foundation. It can absorb living beings into its walls while keeping them alive and preserved.
relics.curse: The citadel consumes those who breach its interior, absorbing them into its structure while maintaining their consciousness. Those absorbed cannot be freed. It screams when wounded.

relics.name: Yarithax's hoard
relics.origin: Accumulated by the Lich-King Yarithax; origin unknown.
relics.power: Contains an unknown quantity of cursed coins, each bearing the Lich-King's profile and the inscription 'This debt is not yet settled.' The coins create magical debts that compound daily.
relics.curse: Any coin spent creates a debt that grows exponentially and cannot be fully repaid by conventional means; only service to the Grey Collectors can partially discharge the debt, and the remainder compounds indefinitely.

relics.name: The Plague-Bringer's Crown
relics.origin: Unknown antiquity; sealed in vault beneath Ostern.
relics.power: Grants overwhelming presence and power, enabling victory in desperate circumstances. Causes the wearer to become a figure of absolute dread.
relics.curse: Wearing it inverts the shadow, causes erratic behavior, sleeplessness, and permanent spiritual corruption. At least four previous wearers died from prolonged exposure. Even thirty seconds of wearing corrupts for years.

relics.name: blood-stone
relics.origin: Unknown; used in Thysara's palace for midnight sorceries.
relics.power: Binds victims to ritual space; serves as anchor for blood-sorcery ceremonies.
relics.curse: Those bound to it are enslaved to the ritual's purpose.

relics.name: Elder Stones
relics.origin: Unknown; stand in the Wastes of Ashemark, ancient beyond reckoning.
relics.power: Grant communion with dying constellations; convey cosmic knowledge about the cyclical nature of stars.
relics.curse: Those who touch them receive overwhelming knowledge; the apothecary's warning about the Heartstone suggests forbidden magics in this region demand payment.

relics.name: The Lamia's Pearl
relics.origin: Ancient artifact guarded by the Lamia in the Sunken Caverns.
relics.power: Grants control over water itself.
relics.curse: None apparent; the pearl was never claimed or tested.

relics.name: Morgath's Bound Power
relics.origin: Morgath's own essence, bound into the earth through his final ritual.
relics.power: Contains the full power of the great necromancer Morgath, bound to prevent necromantic magic from ever being used again.
relics.curse: The power is sealed away forever; attempting to access it would require undoing the very ritual that created it.

relics.name: Shadow of the High Magistrate
relics.origin: Severed by Kael the Nameless from the High Magistrate via shadow-broking
relics.power: An autonomous shadow entity that hungers for stolen identities and memories. Consumed Kael's stolen selves over seven days and eventually possessed him entirely.
relics.curse: Unquenchable hunger; consumes the thief's identities; compounds interest in flesh; possesses the thief as a final vessel.

relics.name: Ossuary (as Living Entity)
relics.origin: Unknown; exists as a natural or magically-created entity
relics.power: Sentient fortress capable of devouring warriors en masse, rearranging its internal architecture in real-time, and possessing living vessels to pilot as extensions of its will.
relics.curse: Those who enter may be devoured or, if chosen, possessed—consciousness trapped while the fortress wears their body.

relics.name: Malachar's Gold
relics.origin: Minted or enchanted by Malachar, a dead lich-king or necromancer.
relics.power: Each coin carries an autonomous supernatural debt that compels the holder into eventual servitude to Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds in blood, loyalty, and life-years daily.
relics.curse: Possession of even one coin triggers supernatural debt collection. The collectors will take soldiers sworn to the holder, then horses/resources, then extract life-years from the holder themselves. The only escape is to accept servitude as a collector enforcing the same debt on others. The debt is eternal and cannot be repaid in gold.

relics.name: merchant's shadow
relics.origin: Stolen years before the story from an unnamed merchant in Pelimor.
relics.power: Allows the wearer to move through daylight without shadow-betrayal. However, the shadow retains the memory and grief of its original owner.
relics.curse: The shadow's retained memories and desire for revenge manifest involuntarily, forcing the wearer's body toward the shadow's enemies. It demands vengeance and cannot be controlled.

regions.name: Sunken Marches
regions.ruler: none (formerly Velgrim the Terrible, now released to peaceful death)

regions.name: Border Lands
regions.ruler: The Crown (nominally); contested by the Rot-King

regions.name: Crimson Vale
regions.function: A territory capable of supporting a warlord's personal dominion with hired soldiers and horses. Now serves as a locus for Malachar's curse collection.
regions.notes: Established as Soroth's domain after he found Malachar's gold. Prospered for three months before collectors arrived. Now ruled by the cursed warlord Soroth who collects debts from others.

regions.name: Pelimor
regions.function: A criminal underworld center and night market hub where stolen goods—including shadows—are bought, sold, and stored in vaults. A place where thieves of high caliber operate.
regions.notes: Pelimor's night markets and vaults are integral to the shadow-theft economy. Mira operated here for years.

regions.name: Drowned Hollows
regions.function: A rural community rooted in the old ways and knowledge of ancient songs. Center of the Rot-King's plague.
regions.notes: Home to Kess and survivors of the plague. The village still practices bone-singing and old magic traditions.

regions.name: The Shattered West
regions.function: A region of broken terrain and mountain ranges, home to both dwarven holds and goblin warrens in a complex negotiation of coexistence. The Shattered West serves as a frontier of underground civilization and surface strongholds.
regions.notes: The Spire of Blackstone and the Warrens represent a delicate balance in this region. Further conflicts or alliances may reshape the Shattered West's political and physical landscape.

substances.name: plague (septic rot)
substances.properties: A plague that blackens victims with septic rot, causing slow death. Affects primarily children and mothers in afflicted villages.
substances.use: The plague ravaged Carrion's Rest until lifted by the Witch of Ravenmoor's curse upon Sister Kess.

substances.name: Potion of Youth
substances.properties: Restores youth and vitality; progressively transforms the consumer into an inhuman predator; hunger for additional victims increases over time; corrupts morality and nature.
substances.use: Consumed as a draught; consumption of others' flesh required for its creation.

substances.name: Stolen Water-Magic
substances.properties: Grants the ability to walk on waves, summon floods, and speak to drowning men. Binds the user to ocean and death in equal measure. Cannot be stolen with impunity; the debt will eventually come due.
substances.use: Used by Nara the Tide-Thief for three years before the debt caught up with her.

substances.name: blood-ink
substances.properties: Used to inscribe daemon-binding contracts on parchment; grants power but binds the soul.

substances.name: Grafted Metal
substances.properties: Can be grafted to replace lost flesh; restores physical form but without sensation or full function.
substances.use: Prosthetic replacement for lost limbs in plague victims.

substances.name: Lamia's Tears
substances.properties: Luminescent; possesses water-magic power; capable of reversing environmental catastrophe and bringing abundance.
substances.use: Used to sustain Torven's village and reverse the effects of receding seas.

substances.name: Emerald of Khar-Zul
substances.properties: Nine emeralds of immense monetary value; each is worth more than most kingdoms. They are warm to the touch and serve as conduits for curse magic when removed from the idol.
substances.use: Valuable trade goods; components of the idol of Khar-Zul's protective magic.

substances.name: Scarlet Powders
substances.use: Sold as forbidden trade goods by Theron; could be weaponized or used for enhancement.

substances.name: Vision Oils
substances.use: Sold as forbidden trade goods by Theron; likely used for divination or mystical experience.

substances.name: Slow-Acting Saps
substances.properties: Killed slowly and invisibly, making them ideal for undetectable murder.
substances.use: Sold as forbidden trade goods by Theron; used by the young woman to kill her child.

substances.name: Blood-Rust
substances.properties: A corruption that causes metal to corrode and decay as if infected with blood. Every coin Theron earned turned to blood-rust in his purse.
substances.use: A manifestation of curse-magic; cannot be deliberately crafted but occurs as a curse effect.

substances.name: stolen shadows
substances.properties: Unknown magical properties; untraced and untraceable; can be stored in clay jars; some are inhabited by malevolent entities.
substances.use: Traded in night markets as high-value illicit goods. Can be weaponized against their original owners if inhabited by malevolent forces.

substances.name: Malachar's Gold
substances.properties: Stamped with a skeletal crown. Carries autonomous supernatural debt. Compels holder into servitude.
substances.use: Intended as currency or treasure, but functions as a curse-delivery mechanism. Cannot be safely used as currency due to debt activation.

substances.name: septic rot
substances.properties: A manifestation of plague that blackens the skin of victims. Caused by the Rot-King. Spreads through a village rapidly.
substances.use: A curse or plague weapon; not used intentionally by mortals.

substances.name: Restoration Honey
substances.tagline: An elixir that unmakes death itself
substances.status: Known to exist; dangerous and unpredictable
